taxonomy: Choritaenia capensis is classified within the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is a type of broomrape, a group of holoparasitic flowering plants. Choritaenia capensis is native to South Africa and belongs to the genus Choritaenia, which is part of the order Lamiales. The family Orobanchaceae includes approximately 200 species of plants, many of which are parasitic. Choritaenia capensis is a holoparasitic plant, meaning it obtains all its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants.
